message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step approach:
1) Mapped each main REG node to its specific GDPR requirement and identified the 1-hop cross-references explicitly mentioned in the node text:
- REG-086 (Art49(1)) references adequacy decisions under Art45(3) and appropriate safeguards under Art46 (paras 1–5).
- REG-022 (Art15(2)) references “appropriate safeguards pursuant to Article 46,” which includes Art46(1)–(5).
- REG-069 (Art45(1)) logically ties to Art45(3) (adequacy decision implementing act details).
- REG-078 (Art46(1)) explicitly references Art45(3) and links to Art46(2)–(5).
- REG-080 (Art46(3)) ties to Art46(1) and Art46(4).

2) Read the policy chunk carefully for any explicit statements on:
- International transfers (EEA to third countries).
- Adequacy decisions (Art45).
- Appropriate safeguards and their forms (Art46).
- Derogations (Art49).
- Data subject right to be informed of safeguards (Art15(2)).

3) Matched policy text to each REG requirement:
- The policy states international transfers occur with “an appropriate level of data protection … ensured by means of suitable security measures.” It does not specify adequacy decisions, specific Art46 mechanisms (SCCs, BCRs, codes, certification), SA authorization under Art46(3), or Art49 derogations. It also does not describe the Art15(2) right to be informed of safeguards.
- Per instruction 4, omissions must be treated as Non-Deviation unless there is a direct contradiction, narrowing, over-compliance, or other deviation type that can be evidenced by quotes from both GDPR and policy.

4) Determined deviation status for each main node:
- REG-069 (Art45(1)): No contradiction with adequacy transfers; the policy’s general “security measures” language does not negate adequacy-based transfers. Absence of adequacy mention is an omission, which must be Non-Deviation.
- REG-078 (Art46(1)): Policy’s “suitable security measures” is high-level and does not contradict the requirement for “appropriate safeguards” plus enforceable rights/remedies; absence of these details is an omission, treated as Non-Deviation.
- REG-080 (Art46(3)): No statement contradicting SA authorization or specific mechanisms; omissions only. Non-Deviation.
- REG-086 (Art49(1)): No claim to rely on derogations contrary to the Article; omissions only. Non-Deviation.
- REG-022 (Art15(2)): The right to be informed of Art46 safeguards is not mentioned; omission only. Non-Deviation.

5) Conclusion:
- For all five main nodes, there is no explicit deviation detectable in this chunk. All missing specifics are omissions and must be classified as Non-Deviation under the provided rules.
</deliberation>
